title: salix gracilistyla f. adscendens
intro: Salix gracilistyla f. adscendens, also known as the 'Hime Shirakashi' or 'Korean weeping willow', is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Salix gracilistyla f. adscendens include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
